title: peltophorus divergens
intro: Peltophorus divergens, also known as the lead tree or ironwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as being a natural source of tannins and alkaloids. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to support immune function, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
